paper.title: Base editing of haematopoietic stem cells rescues sickle cell disease in mice

formulations.targeting_details: Ex vivo electroporation of ABE8e-NRCH mRNA + sgRNA into CD34+ HSPCs. NOT an LNP delivery paper.
formulations.formulation_notes: This is an EX VIVO paper, not in vivo LNP delivery. Included for establishing therapeutic thresholds, not for formulation data.

experiments.strain_or_model: SCD patient CD34+ HSPCs → xenotransplant into immunodeficient mice

safety.tolerability_notes: Base editing avoided p53 activation and large deletions observed with Cas9 nuclease.

experiments.notes: 80% HBBS-to-HBBG conversion ex vivo in patient HSPCs. 68% HBBG frequency maintained at 16 weeks post-transplant. 5-fold decrease in hypoxia-induced sickling. This establishes the benchmark for ex vivo base editing efficiency.

experiments.strain_or_model: Townes SCD mouse HSPCs → transplant into irradiated recipients

results.hsc_definition: Mouse HSPCs from Townes SCD model

safety.tolerability_notes: Near-normal haematological parameters. Reduced splenic pathology.

experiments.notes: Makassar beta-globin = 79% of total beta-globin protein at 16 weeks. 3-fold reduction in hypoxia-induced sickling. Near-normal blood parameters.

experiments.strain_or_model: Townes SCD mouse → secondary transplant with varying edit proportions
experiments.notes: KEY FINDING: ≥20% HBBS-to-HBBG editing is SUFFICIENT for phenotypic rescue. Established via secondary transplant with mixed edited/unedited BM. This 20% threshold is now THE reference point for all in vivo HSC editing approaches including Tessera, Editas, and your Pareto analysis.

key_findings_for_project: ESTABLISHES THE 20% THERAPEUTIC THRESHOLD: ≥20% HBBS→HBBG editing is sufficient for phenotypic rescue of SCD
80% ex vivo editing efficiency in patient HSPCs — benchmark for what's achievable with optimized base editor
68% maintained at 16 weeks post-transplant — durable in LT-HSCs
Makassar (HBBG) is the same edit that Tessera and Lian are targeting in vivo
Secondary transplant with varying proportions establishes dose-response for therapeutic benefit
Base editing avoids p53 activation and large deletions vs Cas9 — safety advantage
Same David Liu + Gregory Newby + Mitchell Weiss team as Lian 2024 LNP paper
Ex vivo, NOT in vivo — but defines the target for all in vivo approaches

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Breda's ~6% editing is below the 20% threshold established here. Breda needs to improve.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Shi's reporter delivery doesn't directly translate to editing %. But 20% editing threshold means Shi's delivery efficiency needs to be substantially higher than 20% to account for reporter-to-editing gap.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Kim's barcoded delivery screen measures delivery, not editing. This paper shows that delivery → editing conversion is not 1:1.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: Not applicable — ex vivo, no LNP formulation, no Pareto placement.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: DEFINES the therapeutic window: ≥20% edited HSCs = phenotypic rescue. Tessera's 24-40% NHP HBB editing exceeds this. Your Pareto analysis should use 20% as the minimum efficacy threshold line.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Zero formulation relevance (ex vivo electroporation). Pure therapeutic benchmark.

limitations_and_caveats: Ex vivo electroporation — not in vivo LNP delivery
Myeloablative conditioning (irradiation) required for transplant — this is what in vivo approaches aim to avoid
Mouse model (Townes) may not perfectly recapitulate human SCD
20% threshold established in mouse model — human threshold may differ
ABE8e-NRCH is a specific base editor; efficiency may differ with other editing modalities
Makassar (HBBG) is benign but NOT wild-type — unknown if fully equivalent long-term
